summary: Israeli forces intensified artillery and air strikes on multiple towns in southern Lebanon, including Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah, while IDF units remain deployed up to 10 km inside Lebanese territory. Hezbollah reported successfully targeting an Israeli vehicle, and a senior Israeli officer, Res‑Har (mil.) Alexander Filin, was killed with seven others wounded in a blast in southern Lebanon. The United States and Iran signed a Memorandum of Understanding, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, lifting the naval blockade and commencing mine‑clearance operations.
key_points: Israeli artillery struck Kfar Jouz in the Nabatieh district of southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194662).
Israeli airstrikes hit the southern Lebanese towns of Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah (Source: @presstv/194668).
Hezbollah fighters claimed to have hit an Israeli Defense Forces vehicle during the night (Source: @presstv/194665).
Res‑Har (mil.) Alexander Filin, 29, was killed and seven soldiers were injured after an explosive device detonated during a foot patrol in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17504).
IDF announced its operational zone extends approximately 10 km inside Lebanese territory, focusing on threat removal and civilian protection (Source: @New_security8200/201962).
The United States and Iran signed a Memorandum of Understanding reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the naval blockade, with mine‑clearance still ongoing (Source: @shin_persian/7124).
UKMTO issued an advisory that the Strait of Hormuz is open and blockade operations have ceased, noting active mine‑clearance (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4104).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli forces intensified artillery and air strikes on Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah in southern Lebanon, while IDF units remain deployed up to 10 km inside Lebanese territory. Hezbollah reported a successful attack on an Israeli vehicle, and a senior Israeli officer, Alexander Filin, was killed with seven others wounded in a blast. The US‑Iran Memorandum of Understanding reopened the Strait of Hormuz, lifted the naval blockade and initiated mine‑clearance operations.

points: The United States and Iran signed a Memorandum of Understanding that immediately reopened the Strait of Hormuz and lifted the naval blockade, with commercial shipping resuming toll‑free for 60 days (Source: @shin_persian/7124).
The agreement includes a 60‑day period during which no fees will be charged to vessels transiting the Strait, with costs covered by the Iranian government (Source: @VahidOnline/76494).
Mine‑clearance operations are ongoing in the Strait of Hormuz following the reopening (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4104).
Iranian officials indicated that the MOU does not permit the development of certain missile types, reflecting US security concerns (Source: @yediotnews25/131619).
Israeli artillery struck the town of Kfar Jouz in the Nabatieh district of southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194662).
Airstrikes were conducted against the southern Lebanese towns of Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah (Source: @presstv/194668).
Hezbollah fighters claimed to have successfully targeted an Israeli Defense Forces vehicle during the night (Source: @presstv/194665).
Res‑Har (mil.) Alexander Filin, 29, was killed and seven soldiers were injured after an explosive device detonated during a foot patrol in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17504).
The IDF maintains a security zone extending roughly 10 km inside Lebanese territory, focusing on threat removal and protection of northern Israeli civilians (Source: @New_security8200/201962).
No specific operational updates regarding Gaza or the West Bank were present in the source material.
Israeli artillery and airstrikes targeted Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194662, @presstv/194668).
Hezbollah reported a successful attack on an Israeli vehicle, indicating ongoing low‑intensity clashes (Source: @presstv/194665).
A senior Israeli officer, Alexander Filin, was killed and seven others wounded by an explosive device during a foot patrol in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17504).
IDF forces are operating up to 10 km inside Lebanese territory as part of a security zone to neutralize threats (Source: @New_security8200/201962).
Additional reports mention continued air‑defense alerts and missile launches toward Israeli positions in the Kfar‑Tebnit area (Source: @redlinkleb/26641).
No specific operational updates regarding Syria or Iraq were present in the source material.
The US‑Iran MOU led to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the cessation of the US naval blockade, affecting Gulf maritime traffic (Source: @shin_persian/7124, @Faytuks_Network/4104).
UKMTO advisory confirmed the Strait is open and that mine‑clearance activities are ongoing (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4104).

key_developments: Israeli artillery struck Kfar Jouz in southern Lebanon, marking a significant escalation of cross‑border fire (Source: @presstv/194662).
Airstrikes on Kfar Jouz, Harouf, and Al‑Sharqiyah demonstrated expanded Israeli air operations in Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194668).
Hezbollah claimed a successful hit on an Israeli vehicle, indicating active resistance capabilities (Source: @presstv/194665).
The death of Res‑Har (mil.) Alexander Filin and injuries to seven soldiers highlight the lethal risk to IDF patrols (Source: @almogboker78/17504).
IDF’s security zone now extends roughly 10 km inside Lebanese territory, showing a deeper operational footprint (Source: @New_security8200/201962).
The US‑Iran Memorandum of Understanding reopened the Strait of Hormuz and lifted the naval blockade, altering regional maritime dynamics (Source: @shin_persian/7124).
UKMTO advisory confirmed the Strait of Hormuz is open with ongoing mine‑clearance, confirming the MOU’s implementation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4104).
sections.threat_assessment: Israeli strikes and artillery in southern Lebanon, combined with Hezbollah’s reported vehicle attack, indicate a heightened low‑intensity conflict along the Israel‑Lebanon border. The death of a senior Israeli officer underscores the vulnerability of IDF patrols to improvised explosive devices. The US‑Iran MOU reduces maritime tension but does not eliminate the risk of asymmetric attacks in the region, especially given ongoing mine‑clearance operations. Continued Israeli presence 10 km inside Lebanon may provoke further retaliatory actions from Hezbollah and other militia groups.
sections.regional_response: Iran’s agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade reflects a diplomatic shift aimed at easing economic pressure, yet Iranian statements emphasize continued resistance to certain missile developments. Gulf states are monitoring the maritime reopening while maintaining vigilance over mine‑clearance progress. Hezbollah’s public claim of striking an Israeli vehicle suggests a willingness to sustain armed resistance despite diplomatic overtures. International maritime agencies, such as UKMTO, have updated advisories to reflect the changed security environment.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future monitoring should focus on IDF operational depth in southern Lebanon and potential escalation triggers, including further Hezbollah attacks on Israeli vehicles or personnel. Mine‑clearance status in the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical indicator of maritime safety and compliance with the MOU. Iranian domestic enforcement actions, such as arrests of cultural figures, may signal internal security tightening that could affect external diplomatic posture. Continued analysis of US‑Iran communications will be essential to gauge the durability of the maritime agreement.
